chunk_summary: Guidelines for safe spacing and documentation of national prophylaxis doses, including Vitamin A, IFA, and Albendazole, to prevent unsafe repeat dosing and ensure effective population-level prevention in clinics.
learning_objectives: Understand the age-based Vitamin A prophylaxis dosing schedule and spacing rules.
Learn the guidelines for IFA prophylaxis and therapeutic iron supplementation.
Identify safety protocols for Albendazole administration under National Deworming Day (NDD).
Recognize the importance of documentation and verification to prevent dosing errors.
key_facts: Vitamin A prophylaxis: 9 months (100,000 IU), 16–18 months (200,000 IU), then every 6 months until 5 years (total nine doses).

IFA prophylaxis: 6–59 months (biweekly syrup: 20 mg iron + 100 mcg folic acid), 5–10 years (weekly tablet: 45 mg iron + 400 mcg folic acid), 10–19 years (weekly tablet: 60 mg iron + 500 mcg folic acid).
Albendazole dosing: 200 mg for 1–2 years, 400 mg for ≥2 years; not routine below 1 year.
Minimum 6-month interval between Vitamin A doses after 16–18 months; defer if last dose is uncertain or within 6 months.
Prophylactic IFA should be paused during therapeutic iron treatment and restarted post-treatment with follow-up.
reference_quote: Safety hinges on documentation and spacing. Unified clinic rule when the last dose is uncertain: after the 16–18 month dose, maintain a minimum 6-month interval.
document_key_topics: Vitamin A prophylaxis dosing and spacing
Documentation and verification protocols in clinics
